seoTitle: Douglas Park vs Dover Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Douglas Park and Dover Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Douglas Park vs Dover Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Douglas Park and Dover Heights.
heading: Douglas Park vs Dover Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Douglas Park, New South Wales and Dover Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Douglas Park has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Dover Heights (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Douglas Park or Dover Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Douglas Park has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Dover Heights scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Douglas Park scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Dover Heights scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Douglas Park or Dover Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Douglas Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
